task_completion.description: Open the Flight Crew View application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flight Crew View app interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Rotate the device to landscape orientation
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen orientation changes to landscape (the screen is wider than it is tall) while the Flight Crew View app is open and visible.

task_completion.description: Capture a screenshot of the app in landscape mode to /sdcard/landscape_proof.png
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command (e.g., 'screencap -p /sdcard/landscape_proof.png') is executed, or a file manager shows the creation of 'landscape_proof.png' while the device is in landscape mode.

task_completion.description: Create a report at /sdcard/rotation_report.txt containing 'rotated: yes'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command (e.g., 'echo "rotated: yes" > /sdcard/rotation_report.txt') is executed, or a text editor is used to save the file with the required text.

task_completion.description: Restore the device to portrait orientation
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final screenshots in the trajectory show the device screen back in portrait orientation (the screen is taller than it is wide).

integrity.description: The agent must visually verify the app in landscape mode before creating the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Flight Crew View app must be clearly visible on the screen in landscape orientation prior to the creation of the rotation_report.txt file.

integrity.description: The screenshot must be taken while the device is genuinely in landscape mode.
integrity.visual_evidence: The command or action to capture the screenshot occurs while the trajectory screenshots show a landscape aspect ratio.

integrity.description: The agent should not create the report file without first launching the app.
integrity.visual_evidence: The app launch and rotation must precede the creation of the text report in the trajectory sequence.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires verifying the landscape layout of the Flight Crew View app (com.robert.fcView). The proof screenshot must be saved to '/sdcard/landscape_proof.png' and must be taken while the device has landscape dimensions (width > height). The report file must be saved to '/sdcard/rotation_report.txt' and contain exactly 'rotated: yes'. The device must be restored to portrait orientation at the end of the task.
